page.title: Linking Words for Reason, Result and Contrast
page.subtitle_html: Linking Words for Reason, Result and Contrast — B2 linking words for reason, result and contrast in clear connected sentences.
page.subtitle_text: B2 linking words for reason, result and contrast in clear connected sentences.
tags: Linking Words for Reason, Result and Contrast

blocks.body: {"level":"B2","intro":"Linking Words for Reason, Result and Contrast: Linking Words for Reason, Result and Contrast focuses on because of, due to, therefore, as a result, although, despite, and however.","rules":[{"label":"Reason","text":"Use because of or due to before nouns and noun phrases.","example":"Because of the traffic, we arrived late."},{"label":"Result","text":"Use so, therefore, or as a result to show consequence.","example":"It was late, so we took a taxi."},{"label":"Contrast","text":"Use although, despite, however, or nevertheless for contrast.","example":"Although it was cold, we went out."}]}

blocks.heading: B2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English B2 lesson package.
